concerns.analysis: The source PDF features the prominent agency seal/logo for the North Carolina Marine Fisheries on the first page. In the HTML output, this image is completely missing as a visual element; it was replaced with a plain text placeholder instead of a proper HTML tag.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Large circular logo for 'North Carolina Marine Fisheries' in the top-left corner of page 1.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries celebrating 200 years of management and conservation (1822-2022)]

concerns.analysis: The source document has a single continuous top-level ordered list using Roman numerals (I. SIZE LIMIT, II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS, III. GEAR RESTRICTIONS, IV. GENERAL INFORMATION). In the JSON output, items I, II, and III are properly captured as items in an ordered `list` object on Page 1. However, on Page 2, item 'IV. GENERAL INFORMATION' is incorrectly converted into a level 2 `heading` rather than continuing as a list item in the Roman numeral list. This breaks the structural list outlin

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 3 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=3, examples=['https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'http://www.ncmarinefisheries.net/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p2→p3: ordered list J→K continues
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 1 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p3: "...ision of Marine Fisheries, including an address in"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=1 (truncated=1, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document makes extensive use of underlining for emphasis, specifically on primary list headers (e.g., 'SIZE LIMIT', 'HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS', 'GEAR RESTRICTIONS') and entire regulatory paragraphs (e.g., all sub-items under Section II, and Item M on page 3). This underlining is completely lost in the output JSON, which represents these items as plain text within list elements without any emphasis markers.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Underlined text: "HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS" and "M. This proclamation supersedes Proclamation FF-4-2023..."
concerns.output_quote: Plain text: "II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS" and "M. This proclamation supersedes Proclamation FF-4-2023..."
concerns.location: Page 1, Sections I-III; Page 3, Section IV.M

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries
3441
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
